_doc: Tier-1 card fill vectors (design 2026-07-23-card-autofill-tier1 §11). `tables` is the NORMATIVE copy of the brand-synonym / contains-word / month tables — each engine deep-equals its compiled-in tables against these, so drift on either side reds that side ([T14]). `selectIndexShared` = label-only <option> lists (options as plain strings; [T8] pins opt.value's attr-absent→text fallback, so the value pass ≡ the text pass and one string per option is lossless) — BOTH engines run these through their select matcher; `expected` is the 0-based option index, null = no match (safe miss, never a guess). Matching is PASS-MAJOR ([T2]): pass 1 whole-parse, 2 digit-extraction, 3 month-name (FIRST maximal ASCII-letter run, lowercased, EQUALITY against fullName/abbreviation — prefix matching forbidden, [T3]), 4 combined-expiry digits, 5 cc-type synonym-exact-else-contains-primary; the earliest pass with any match wins, options in document order within a pass. `selectIndexDom` = value≠text option pairs ({value,text}; within an option value is checked before text) — TS engine ONLY (Android exposes one string per option). `expiryText`/`yearText`/`monthText` = the §4 text-target adapters; stored halves are canonical (expMonth 'MM', expYear 4-digit); maxLength null = undeclared (DOM -1); any case with a non-null placeholder carries tsOnly:true (core has no placeholder signal — its §9 table is exactly the placeholder-null slice). The [T16] separator comes ONLY from the anchored /m{1,2}\s*([-/.])\s*y/i sniff on the placeholder (default '/') — never a bare contains-scan. cc-type cases carry the stored number AND its derived brand (consistent by CardNormalize.brand — each engine consumes whichever its API takes). Tier-2 (design 2026-07-23-card-autofill-tier2 §1.3 [U8]): `tables.keywords` is the ORDERED [{kind, keywords}] group array — the normative name/id vocabulary; group order is load-bearing (month/year groups before the generic exp group; the TRAILING [U1] {cc-number: [creditcard]} group fires only when no specific kind matched, so bare credit_card → number while credit_card_cvv/_expiry/_type keep their specific verdicts) and BOTH engines assert SEQUENCE equality against it — kinds AND order AND contents, never per-key lookups (a per-key assert stays vacuously green on a reorder). `dateLeg` = CORE-ONLY consumer (the Android CC_EXP AUTOFILL_TYPE_DATE leg, §5): expectedMs is the epoch ms of (expYear, expMonth, day 1) UTC via days-from-civil. `splitPan` = SHARED dual-consumer since Tier 3 [W2] — BOTH engines assert the SAME chunk arrays (core mirrors ext `splitPan` as `CardNormalize.splitPanChunks`): boxes = declared maxLengths in document order (null = undeclared); a split needs >1 box, every box declared 1..8, AND sum ≥ PAN length → sequential digit chunks, final box a short remainder; a chunk is "" ONLY when earlier boxes already consumed every digit ([8,8,8]/15 → box3 "", whereas [4,4,4,4]/15 → a 3-digit last chunk); expected null = no split (one box, an undeclared box, or sum < digits — the whole-PAN fallback + fit-guard governs). The read-back `verifySplitPanLanded` stays the only ext-only split piece. `splitPanPlan` = CORE-ONLY consumer [W1] (the CardFill.plan integration): `clusters[{frameDomain, boxes[{kind, autofillType, maxLength}]}]` flatten to CcFields in order (box index = position across ALL clusters' boxes); `autofillType` is the CardFill.AUTOFILL_TYPE_* int (1 TEXT / 3 LIST / 4 DATE); `pan`/`expMonth`/`expYear` drive CardData. The >1-CC_NUMBER-TEXT-box split pre-pass runs PER frame cluster AFTER the anchor gate, so a cluster with no CC_NUMBER anchor is zero-filled; an ineligible (undeclared-maxLength) >1-box shape fills the FIRST box whole-PAN and SUPPRESSES the rest ([W1]); a single CC_NUMBER box is unchanged (whole PAN — the design's "single-box → whole PAN" lives here in the plan, not in the pure `splitPan`, which returns null for one box). `expectedPlanned` = [{index, text}] for the filled TEXT boxes ONLY; a box absent from it is SKIPPED. dateLeg/splitPanPlan are whole CORE-ONLY sections; splitPan/tables are dual — the tsOnly flag has no inverse, so a mixed single-consumer section would red the wrong engine. `tables.asciiFold` = the shared [W3] char→STRING digraph fold (explicit, NOT normalize("NFD")), asserted by BOTH engines; the non-decomposables (ß ø æ œ ł đ ð þ) are the pin that reds an NFD-style fork, uppercase forms map identically, unmapped chars pass through. `tables.monthNamesByLocale` = the shared [W5] locale→[12 FOLDED full month names] for de/fr/es/pt/it/nl, authored in the asciiFold alphabet (März→maerz — the design doc agrees since the review-fold (entries MUST be fold-stable — the matcher folds the page option with the same table); authored maerz so a folded "März" option actually matches), asserted by BOTH engines and consulted post-fold in the month-name select pass only (zero cross-month collisions vs every listed locale + en, verified). `radioIndexFor` = EXT-ONLY [W9]/[T14] (V3 radio card-type): {options:[{value,text}], brand} → index|null, the pure cc-type matcher (synonym-exact across all options, then contains-primary) reused for a radio group. `postalText` = SHARED G3 [X3-A4c] CC_POSTAL fill leg (both engines consume it, the non-vacuity pin for the new CardFill.textFor / deriveCardWrite branch): a stored postalCode fills a `target:text` cardpostal box VERBATIM (no digit-strip: postal codes are alphanumeric internationally), fit-guarded (a value longer than maxLength SKIPS, never truncates), a missing/empty postal skips; a `target:select` cardpostal derives no faithful write (safe miss). tables.keywords gains a DISTINCT cc-postal group (billingzip/billingpostal/cardpostal/cardzip/avszip/postalcode) placed AFTER cc-type and BEFORE the trailing bare-creditcard [U1] group so no cross-kind run overlaps (verified: shipping* names never match a postal keyword; the shipping-SUPPRESSOR [X3-A1](i) fires only on a string that first produced a postal verdict, e.g. shippingPostalCode). CC_POSTAL is ANCHOR-GATED: it is login-inert in the bare per-field classifier (urimatch.json standalone stays none) and emerges ONLY in an anchored card form's refine post-pass (cardform.json), so login grouping stays bit-identical.

selectIndexShared.name: padded numeric month list whole-parses
unpadded numeric month list whole-parses
decorated month rows match by digit extraction
digit-bearing placeholder never outranks the real month row (pass-major)
english full month names match by letter-run equality
english month abbreviations match, incl the sept variant
localized month list degrades to a safe miss — marraskuu is november, never march
two-digit year option list
four-digit year option list
year list behind a text placeholder row
combined expiry select matches MMYY digits
combined expiry select matches MMYYYY digits
cc-type label list exact-matches visa
cc-type label list exact-matches the spaced american express label
cc-type value-code list matches mastercard 002
cc-type value-code list matches discover 004
cc-type falls back to contains-primary-word
month list with no parseable rows misses
cc-type list of unknown brands misses
cc-type: brand-enumerating header never outranks the exact row (exact pass sweeps ALL options first)
cc-type: contains-only 'Visa Electron' never outranks the exact 'Visa' below it
V2b positive: German month list fills via the folded locale table (Marz via ae-fold = maerz)
V2b positive: French month list fills (août folds to aout; décembre to decembre)

selectIndexDom.name: cybersource numeric value codes behind brand texts
two-letter value codes match on the value side
one-letter value code matches on the value side
numeric month value behind a decorated text
duplicate values resolve by index off the text pass

expiryText.name: maxLength 5 separates MM/YY with the default slash
maxLength 5 honors the sniffed dash separator
maxLength 7 corroborated by a yyyy placeholder
maxLength 7 corroborated by the space-masked MM / YY placeholder
maxLength 7 without a placeholder takes the chromium MM/YYYY default
maxLength 3 cannot hold any truthful expiry
undeclared maxLength with a yyyy placeholder widens to MM/YYYY
undeclared maxLength honors the sniffed dash in the yyyy branch
anchored sniff ignores the dot in an e.g. placeholder
maxLength 8 falls into the sniff default MM/YY
maxLength 8 with a yyyy placeholder widens to MM/YYYY
a combined target needs BOTH halves

yearText.name: undeclared maxLength defaults to YYYY (deliberate flip of the shipped always-YY)
fit-guard shrinks YYYY to YY when only 3 chars fit
maxLength 1 cannot hold any truthful year
declared maxLength 2 beats a yyyy placeholder

monthText.name: maxLength 1 cannot hold any truthful month — never strip the pad
maxLength 3 fits MM with room to spare

splitPan.name: final box takes the short remainder: amex fifteen across 4-4-4-4
an undeclared-maxLength box disables the split (whole-PAN fallback + fit-guard governs)
empty trailing chunk: [8,8,8] over amex fifteen leaves box3 empty (boxes 1-2 already hold all 15 digits)
sum shortfall disables the split: 4-4-4-4 cannot hold a seventeen-digit PAN
single box -> null from the PURE helper (boxes>1 required; the plan fills a lone box whole via textFor, pinned in splitPanPlan)

splitPanPlan.name: ineligible split ([W1]): two undeclared-maxLength CC_NUMBER TEXT boxes fill box0 whole-PAN, box1 SKIPPED (never full-PAN-into-every-box)
eligible split: four maxLength-4 CC_NUMBER boxes over sixteen digits get per-box chunks (non-vacuity: the fit-guard alone would skip every box)

multi-cluster: an eligible split cluster fills per-box; a hostile 2nd-frame cluster with no CC_NUMBER anchor is zero-filled (pre-pass runs after the anchor gate; the lone CC_EXP would fill 07/27 if the gate leaked)
single CC_NUMBER box is unchanged: it fills the whole PAN (the >1-box split pre-pass never fires)

radioIndexFor.name: value match: a radio whose value is a brand synonym is selected (value checked before label)
label match: the adjacent label carries the brand when the value is an opaque code
contains-primary fallback: 'Pay with Visa' matches when no exact synonym row exists
no match: an unknown brand set checks nothing (safe miss, never a guess)

postalText.name: stored postal fills a cardpostal text target verbatim
fit-guard skips a target narrower than the stored postal (partial beats wrong, never truncate)
a missing postalCode skips (never guesses a postal)
select / non-input skip: a cardpostal <select> derives no faithful write (safe miss, never guessed)
